purpose: Block B5 — single source of truth for cutover-gating item status. The CI test at tests/invariants/cutover-gate.test.ts reads this manifest and asserts every required item is verified=wired-and-exercised before runtime/cutover-flag.ts:CUTOVER_ENABLED can be set to true. Schema v2 adds the `verification` field after audit revealed many items were marked done but not actually exercised in production paths. `status` records whether the work was performed; `verification` records whether the work is doing its job in production. Block E readiness requires status=done AND verification=wired-and-exercised. Phase→Block lineage (the original routing refactor's Phases 0-7 are subsumed): Phase 0 → docs/ROUTING_STATE_MACHINE.md. Phase 1 → routing types + registry + codemod (DONE). Phase 2 → routing dispatcher + matrix tests (DONE, B5). Phase 3 → routing shadow mode (DONE). Phase 5 → v2 spec-edit wart fixes (DONE, FLAG-1..5). Phase 4 → Block E (cutover). Phase 6 → Block F1 (cleanup). Phase 7 → Block F2/F3 (Coder/Reviewer agents).

verification_definitions.burn-in: Work shipped; in legitimate burn-in window collecting real-traffic evidence.
verification_definitions.docs-only: Documentation written. Never exercised against real conditions (no drill, no real onboarding, no real incident).
verification_definitions.infrastructure-only: Code/module/script exists with tests, but is NOT WIRED into production callers, OR is narrower in scope than its title claims. Capability exists but is not yet doing its job.
verification_definitions.wired-and-exercised: Code is in production callers AND tested AND verified to fire by synthetic-violation testing. The capability is actively doing its job.

items.title: Fast-path decision recorded in rewrite map
runArchitectAgentV2 (all 7 branches)
items.verification_note: 100+ tests pass but the V2 runner is NOT wired into production — legacy runArchitectAgent still handles all real architect traffic. Only the shadow wrapper observes.

items.title: Architect escalation must consolidate ALL detected gaps (Bug B fix)
items.verification_note: Surfaced 2026-04-30 driving onboarding. auditPmSpec finds N findings; architect's offer_upstream_revision LLM tool call only puts 1 in the question text — N-1 silently dropped. Caught later only by the deterministic re-audit safety net at message.ts:1062-1080 after a writeback. Means user does N round-trips for what should be one. Fix shape: extend post-run escalation gate at message.ts:3163-3181 to verify the agent's escalation question contains ALL auditPmSpec findings consolidated; if not, override the question text with the consolidated brief from parsePmGapText / formatPmGapNotice. See BACKLOG.md → 'Bug B'.

items.title: PM/Designer briefs must declare readOnly to the agent (Bug C fix)
items.verification_note: Surfaced 2026-04-30. PM in escalation-confirmed mode is readOnly:true (no tool access) but the brief at message.ts:951-964 doesn't tell PM that. PM produces prose like 'Applying the patch to AC 10 now' contradicting the platform's truthful 'reply to confirm' message that follows. Same class as Block N2 (prose-vs-state mismatch) at the brief layer. Fix shape: structural prompt update — add explicit 'you are read-only; do not claim to apply changes' clause to architect→PM and design→PM briefs. Cross-agent invariant test asserts both briefs contain the clause. See BACKLOG.md → 'Bug C'.

items.title: Engineering-spec writeback must respect SSOT (Bug D fix)
items.verification_note: Surfaced 2026-04-30. patchEngineeringSpecWithDecision appends a '### Architect Decision (pre-engineering)' section to the engineering spec on every upstream resolution — duplicate-heading every time. Violates Principle 1 (single source of truth — product spec is authoritative for product decisions). auditSpecStructure already detects the duplicates but the writeback isn't gated. Fix shape: delete patchEngineeringSpecWithDecision entirely (architect re-reads product spec when needed) OR make it idempotent (replace, don't append) AND gate writeback on the structural audit. See BACKLOG.md → 'Bug D'.

items.title: patchProductSpecWithRecommendations must apply category rules deterministically (Bug E fix)
items.verification_note: Surfaced 2026-04-30 step 6. PM gives a category rule like "any 'immediately' becomes 'within 1 second'" expecting find-and-replace across the entire spec. Sonnet's patcher applies inconsistently — replaces some instances, leaves others. Deterministic auditPmSpec catches misses and triggers a re-escalation, but each miss = an extra round trip. Fix shape: when PM's recommendation contains an explicit category rule (regex-detectable), apply the substitution deterministically (string-level) BEFORE handing to Sonnet for the rest of the merge. Or gate the writeback on a deterministic re-audit before postMessage; if the audit still has the same category findings, force another Sonnet pass with stricter directive. See BACKLOG.md → 'Bug E'.

items.title: Platform-built messages must not impersonate the agent in third person (Bug F fix)
items.verification_note: Surfaced 2026-04-30. Re-escalation message at message.ts:1078 formatted as `*Product Manager* — Spec partially updated, but N gap remains. Say *yes* to bring the PM agent back.` Prefixes platform's text with agent name (as if PM said it) while content talks about PM in third person. Voice/perspective inconsistency. Same pattern likely exists in other platform-composed messages with agent-name prefix. Fix shape: either (a) drop agent-name prefix on PLATFORM-built messages (use neutral `*Platform —*` or no prefix), or (b) rewrite body in first person consistent with prefix. Audit all platform-built messages in handlers/message.ts. Cross-agent invariant test asserts no platform-built message uses third-person about the agent it's prefixed as. See BACKLOG.md → 'Bug F'.

items.title: Spec-content verifier — no agent recommendation passes without verifying cited content exists (architectural gap; covers Bug G)
items.verification_note: Surfaced 2026-04-30. Today's only defense against agent hallucination of spec content (AC numbers, screen names, section headings) is user inspection. Without a structural verifier, every PM/architect/designer escalation/recommendation is a hallucination waiting to slip past. Bug G (PM cites wrong AC numbers in escalation-resume) is one manifestation; same class can occur in architect (citing engineering AC, design screens) and designer (citing brand tokens, screens). Fix shape: new module runtime/spec-content-verifier.ts exporting verifyContentReferences(agentResponse, specContext): { hallucinations[] }. Called as a post-LLM enforcement gate at every agent's response site. When hallucinations detected: re-prompt the agent with 'you cited X but actual content is Y — fix and resend.' Same architectural pattern as Block N's enforceNoHedging. Cross-agent invariant test asserts every agent's response site calls this verifier. Until this lands, user is the only defense against spec-content hallucinations. See BACKLOG.md → 'Architectural gap' + 'Bug G'.

items.title: PM hallucinates AC numbers / fabricates spec content (Bug G fix — subsumed by B11)
items.verification_note: Surfaced 2026-04-30 across multiple rounds (1:08 PM cited AC 8 as containing 'after inactivity' — it doesn't; 4:14 PM cited AC 27 as containing 'immediately' — it doesn't). PM in escalation-resume mode free-writes prose recommending patches against AC numbers it picks without verifying the AC actually contains the wording. Subsumed by B11 (the platform-wide content verifier covers PM as one case). Tracked separately for traceability to the specific PM-side instance. See BACKLOG.md → 'Bug G'.

items.title: Multi-tenant correctness verification (sandbox tenant-B parallel run)
Pre-cutover migration smoke against prod state
Nightly E2E smoke (real GitHub + real Slack)
Real-fixture producer tests for every classifier
Error-path coverage (GitHub 502, Slack rate-limit, Anthropic 529, GitHub 401)
State-corruption recovery (partial write, restart race, unknown fields)
Chaos / fuzzing (message ordering, mid-turn restart, concurrent spec edits)
Escalation notifications must survive bot restart within TTL (Bug A fix)
items.verification_note: Surfaced 2026-04-30. conversation-store.ts:308-310 deliberately clears all escalationNotifications on every startup. Asymmetric with pendingEscalations (which use timestamp + clearStaleEntries with TTL). Result: every CODE_MARKER bump → required restart → in-flight escalation lost → user redoes the round. J3 marker bump and this clear-on-restart are two enforcements that collide. Fix shape: add timestamp to EscalationNotification type, replace clear-all-on-restart with clearStaleEntries(escalationNotifications, 'escalationNotification'). Same TTL constant as other pending state. See BACKLOG.md → 'Bug A'.

items.title: Log-coverage gate (every state mutation has structured log line)
items.verification_note: Invariant scans only runtime/conversation-store.ts mutators. Misses message.ts post-run mutations (escalation override replacement, hedge-gate skip path), tool-handler state mutations, github-client state writes. The G1 spirit demands platform-wide; the implementation is one file.

items.title: Metrics emission (counters + histograms via thin interface)
Alert hooks (cutover divergence, smoke failure, perf budget breach)
Log retention + redaction (PII/token scrubbing, per-tenant tagging)
items.verification_note: redactToken/redactPii/redactAll module + 12 unit tests, but ZERO callers in production code. The bot's logger does not redact. Real GitHub responses still get logged in plaintext.

items.title: Canonical eval dataset (50+ per agent, 30+ per classifier)
Model-upgrade evals (run on Claude version bump)
Coverage thresholds enforced at PR time
Regression catalog (one test file per historical bug)
Per-agent per-behavior MT-N entries in MANUAL_TESTS.md
Per-tenant isolation at storage layer (RLS or equivalent)
Backup strategy (daily snapshot, restore tested)
items.verification_note: Script exists, ran once today to create backup. Restore NEVER tested. Local-only (no off-host). DR drill never performed.

items.title: DR runbook (full state loss, token revocation, outage, host failure)
Admin audit log (immutable, separate from operational logs)
items.verification_note: recordAdminAuditEvent module + 6 unit tests, but ZERO callers in production code. No real event has ever been recorded.

items.title: Tenant offboarding script (GDPR-compliant deletion)
items.verification_note: Script exists with --dry-run; NEVER RUN against real tenant state. The 'delete the GitHub spec files' half is operator-manual, not scripted.

items.verification_note: Script exists; NEVER USED to scaffold a real agent. Template bugs would only surface in real use.
